title: Natural childbirth and having babies at home; her household chores; fears that she had
description: Midwives; natural childbirth. Births of younger sisters and brothers, hiding in the orchard because mother hollered so badly; having her own children. Hauling water, washing clothes at the creek. Fear of the dark; visiting the outhouse in the dark; fear of the train in Troy.

title: Visiting Troy; Bear Creek/Dry Creek school; visiting Carl Olson's family
description: Remembrances of visiting Troy after hiking 9 miles; walking on the top of snow. Bear Creek School (later Dry Creek) and parties; visiting Carl Olson's family; big piles of pancakes for breakfast at home and other foods.

title: Mother's jobs; going to school and learning English; sewing clothes without patterns
description: Mother's jobs; going to school, learning English; mother's intelligence shown in her sewing all of the clothes without patterns. Washing clothes by hand. More on having babies at home.

title: Preparing father for burial, the coffin on the front porch, funeral preparations, no flowers
description: Preparing father for burial, the coffin on the front porch, funeral preparations, no flowers.

title: Going to Parties and dances in empty houses; getting married; working in Spokane
description: Parties and dances in old empty houses; meeting her husband; family pictures; married at 29 - having too much fun to be married earlier; working in Spokane as housekeeper; coming home to help mother for birth of younger brothers and sisters, 13 total including a half-brother and two half-sisters.

title: Walking in Spokane; mother's sewing talents
description: Never any fear to walk in Spokane; more on mother's sewing talent; mother a very quiet woman. Big Anderson.

title: Young people need to work; playing and fishing as a child; Big Anderson and Wild Dave; walking to school
description: Young people, need to go to work; as child, playing, fishing, picking huckleberries; Big Anderson smelled bad; Wild Dave; walking to school through the scary woods.

title: Homemade medicine; going to town for groceries; neighbors getting sick
description: No sickness; homemade medicine; going to town for groceries; more on mother. Different food; sickness and neighbors, measles not considered dangerous; big families.

title: Going to work in Spokane; Going to school and being good at spelling; making $6 a week
description: Going to work in Spokane; never worked hard because of being small; doing tub laundry and the soap. School; fun at recess; good at spelling not at map drawing, so copying from neighbor; need to know enough to get out to work. Making $6 a week.

title: More stories about Big Anderson; traveling many miles to visit others
description: Big Anderson; Food and eating with friends, families would travel many miles to visit overnight.

title: Canning and picking fruit a family project
description: Canning fruit; picking the fruit a family project. Hiking to town.

title: Buildings in Troy that she remembers; father would buy whiskey for medicine
description: The buildings she remembers in town of Troy. Dad would buy whiskey for medicine for colds. Roads were very bad.
